name: Fried Pork Dumplings W/Hot Garlic Sauce
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Cooked Pork - boned, cut into 1-inch squares
1/2 bunch Scallions - cleaned, cut in half
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a knife, coarsely chop pork and scallions in a food processor using short bursts. Add ginger, egg yolk, soy sauce, sesame oil, dry sherry, and lemon pepper. Process for a few short bursts to mix. Set the pork mixture aside on a plate.
2. Rinse and dry the processor bowl and blade.
3. Place flour and MSG in the processor bowl. Using the knife blade, process continuously while adding water all at once. Continue processing until a ball of dough forms in the bowl, or 20 seconds, whichever comes first. Remove the dough and knead until elastic.
4. Using half of the dough, roll out on a lightly floured board to 1/16-inch thick. Cut into 2-inch circles using a biscuit cutter.
5. Place a rounded teaspoon of pork mixture in the center of each dough circle. Fold the circle over the filling, creating a "half moon" turnover. Press edges together lightly with fingers.
6. Place the filled dumplings on an oiled plate.
7. In a 3-quart saucepan, bring 2 quarts of water to boil. Drop the dumplings in boiling water. Allow to boil for one minute after the dumpling returns to the surface of the water.
8. Drain well; replace dumplings on an oiled plate to cool slightly.
9. Meanwhile, heat 1/2 cup cooking oil in a skillet over medium to medium-high heat. Add dumplings and fry, turning often, until lightly brown.
10. Drain on paper towels.
11. Process garlic sauce ingredients using the knife blade until smooth.
12. Serve hot dumplings with garlic sauce. They may also be served with Chinese mustard and/or hoisin sauce.
